title: Company Registration Cost and Process in Bangladesh (2025)
metaDescription: Start your business fast! Contact our corporate lawyer in Uttara, Dhaka for a quick, hassle-free company registration and RJSC filing today.
heroIntro: Registering a company in Bangladesh through the Registrar of Joint Stock Companies and Firms (RJSC) is a structured process. Understanding the real costs — government fees, stamp duties, and professional fees — helps you budget accurately and avoid delays.
toc: Types of Companies You Can Register in Bangladesh
Stamp Duty on Memorandum and Articles

sections.h2: Types of Companies You Can Register in Bangladesh
sections.content:
Under the Companies Act 1994, the most common business structures are: Failing to comply may result in criminal liability in corporate fraud. Additionally, if you are acquiring commercial land for your startup, ensure proper vetting.

  • Private Limited Company (Ltd.): 2–50 shareholders, limited liability. Most popular structure for SMEs and startups.
  • Public Limited Company (PLC): 7+ shareholders, can raise capital from the public, requires more compliance.
  • One-Person Company (OPC): Single shareholder, introduced by amendment — suitable for sole entrepreneurs.
  • Partnership Firm: Registered under the Partnership Act 1932, simpler but with unlimited liability.

A company lawyer in Dhaka will advise which structure suits your business model, tax situation, and future plans.

The RJSC online registration process (via roc.gov.bd):

  1. Name clearance: Apply online for name clearance — BDT 200 fee. Clearance usually takes 1–3 days.
  2. Prepare documents: Memorandum of Association (MOA), Articles of Association (AOA), Form IX (consent of directors), Form XII (particulars of directors).
  3. Pay fees online: RJSC registration fee based on authorised capital (see table below), plus stamp duty on MOA/AOA.
  4. Upload documents: Submit scanned documents through the RJSC online portal.
  5. Receive Certificate of Incorporation: Issued digitally, typically within 2–7 working days of complete submission.
  6. Post-registration: Obtain Trade Licence, TIN/E-TIN, VAT registration, and open a bank account.

RJSC registration fees are based on authorised share capital:

  • Up to BDT 5 lakh: BDT 5,075
  • BDT 5 lakh – BDT 10 lakh: BDT 6,975
  • BDT 10 lakh – BDT 50 lakh: BDT 10,375
  • BDT 50 lakh – BDT 1 crore: BDT 15,675
  • BDT 1 crore – BDT 5 crore: BDT 30,875

These are government fees only. Name clearance (BDT 200) and stamp duties are additional. Fees are updated by circular — confirm current rates at roc.gov.bd before filing.

sections.h2: Stamp Duty on Memorandum and Articles
sections.content:
Stamp duty applies to the Memorandum and Articles of Association under the Stamp Act 1899:

  • MOA stamp duty: Based on authorised capital — typically BDT 2,000–10,000+ for most SMEs.
  • AOA stamp duty: Flat rates, usually BDT 1,000–3,000.

Under-stamped documents will be rejected by RJSC. Buy stamp paper from a licensed vendor and have documents notarised before submission.

With complete, correct documents:

  • Name clearance: 1–3 business days
  • RJSC processing: 3–7 business days
  • Certificate of Incorporation: Issued digitally immediately upon approval
  • Total (typical): 1–2 weeks

Post-registration steps (Trade Licence, TIN, VAT) add another 2–4 weeks. Contact Adv. Shah Alam to handle the entire process on your behalf.

faqs.question: What is the minimum capital required to register a company in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: There is no statutory minimum authorised capital for a private limited company. In practice, most companies register with BDT 1 lakh to BDT 10 lakh to keep registration fees manageable.

faqs.question: Can a foreigner register a company in Bangladesh?
faqs.answer: Yes. Foreign nationals can form a company in Bangladesh, subject to Board of Investment (BIDA) approval for certain sectors. At least one director must have a Bangladesh address for service.

faqs.question: Is a company lawyer required for RJSC registration?
faqs.answer: Not legally required, but strongly recommended. The RJSC rejected many applications due to incorrect MOA/AOA drafting. A corporate lawyer ensures first-time approval.

faqs.question: What is the difference between authorised capital and paid-up capital?
faqs.answer: Authorised capital is the maximum share capital stated in the MOA. Paid-up capital is the amount actually invested by shareholders. Registration fees are based on authorised capital.

faqs.question: Do I need to renew my company registration annually?
faqs.answer: Yes. Companies must file annual returns and audited accounts with RJSC. Failure to file attracts penalties and can result in striking off the company from the register.
